env_axis.name: reweighting
env_axis.shift_mechanism: AMS metric reweighting simulates realistic analysis-time distribution shift

facts: 818,238 simulated ATLAS H→ττ events; AMS metric with reweighting simulating realistic analysis sample.
hypotheses: AMS-optimized classification overfits to the specific reweighting scheme rather than generalizing, because methods tuned to one weight distribution track that distribution's particulars.
Simulator-to-data gaps for HiggsML and LHC-Olympics differ, because ATLAS-specific reconstruction shapes the gap beyond what generic CMS-style events show.
intuitions: A HiggsML retrospective documenting which methods were deployed in real ATLAS analyses likely exists.
Recent (2024-25) ATLAS measurements using HiggsML-era technology with real data probably provide a direct sim-to-real test.
unknowns: AMS is a representational commitment (Asimov significance under a specific signal/background assumption); whether AMS-optimization tracks discovery-utility outside this assumption cannot be determined from the data.
The reweighting implicitly defines 'shift', so whether it captures real analysis-time shift cannot be decided from inside the dataset.
epistemic_state.boundary: Being simulator-generated and limited to a single channel (H→ττ), the dataset is silent on cross-channel transfer.
